title: elaeocarpus bontocensis
intro: Elaeocarpus bontocensis, also known as Bontoc tree, is a plant species native to the Philippines and is a member of the Elaeocarpus genus, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus bontocensis include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Elaeocarpus bontocensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
